## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay, so this is about the absolute number one core strategy that I've used over the years to make millions and millions and millions of dollars and help my students and clients make hundreds and hundreds of millions of dollars. And that number one core strategy, no matter what product or business or niche or business model you're in is to always, always, always be focused on your list and building your list. And by list, I mean an email list. I don't mean your Twitter followers or your Facebook fans or whatever. I'm talking about email. Email is and always has been online the absolute king. And yes, there could be a time in the future somewhere where email isn't king, but it is the king right now. It still has been. I've seen this over and over and over. I've coached so many people in launches and otherwise. And it's like when you post something to social, you might get a few friends sharing it. You might get a few people commenting on it. But when you push the button, the send button on an email, it drives action. It drives clicks. It's just breathtaking when I can, I can hit the send button and literally send thousands of people within minutes to a website. It's just crazy. And I mean, that's the scale I'm playing at right now, but it doesn't matter if you have a hundred people on your email list. There's still a hundred people that have invited you into their inbox, which is a very private place. And with a hundred people, you can create some serious impact. So your number one rule is to always be focused on your list and building your list. The wealth is in the list. Your wealth is in the list. Your business is in the list. Your impact is in the list. You have a list of prospects and a list of clients or customers. And the difference is prospects haven't bought anything from you yet and customers have, clients have. And you'll want to treat those two lists differently. It's a fundamentally a different conversation you have with those two lists. ## [06:02 - 09:03]

there. The thing is, small lists can be really powerful. In fact, on a per name basis, small lists are always more powerful. It's just like a universal law. On a per person basis, 1,000 person list is going to be stronger than 100,000 person list. I've seen this over and over. ## [09:03 - 11:01]

When I started out way back, I was getting one subscriber every four days, and I worked up to one a day, and then I worked really hard, and I got up to four a day, and then I kept on working and working and working. I eventually got that up to 30 a day. The thing about 30 a day, that means 900 a month. 900 a month, that's 10,000 people a year. When I was, that was one newsletter I was publishing, and that newsletter, just 30 a day, I eventually got up to, I published it for years. I eventually got to up to 80,000 people on that list, and that was pretty freaking awesome. It wasn't just from 30 a day. There were some other things I did, but still, it just slow and steady wins the race. Again, the size of the list is not nearly as important as your relationship with the list. Do they open your emails? Do they click? Do they respond? Always relationship trumps list size.
